And Vesper will carry the rook-and the lesson-forward into every negotiation, every contract, every moment when power and care threaten to collapse into the same terrible thing.]</p> <hr> <p>[Back in the present, Vesper&#x27;s pen moves again.]</p> <p>[The rook sits under her palm like a held line.]</p> <p>Avyanna: [careful] Do you miss her.</p> <p>[Vesper&#x27;s throat works.]</p> <p>Vesper: Yes.</p> <p>[A beat.]</p> <p>Vesper: And I don&#x27;t use that to make you smaller.</p> <p>[Avyanna swallows.]</p> <p>Avyanna: Okay.</p> <p>[Vesper returns to the paperwork.]</p> <p>[But she leaves the rook where Avyanna can see it.]</p> </div> * [[Continue to Chapter 10: First Watch->Act1 10 First Watch]] * [[Return to route map->Act 1 Route Map]]<h1>Chapter 10: First Watch</h1> <p class="source-note">Source: 10_first_watch.md</p> <div class="scene-prose"> <p>&gt; **LUMEN THIEF - DUTY ROSTER (AMENDED):** &gt; *New crew assignment: Avyanna Lagrange* &gt; *Status: Training* &gt; *Initial duties: Observation, inventory, message running* &gt; *Note: Nothing beyond current physical capacity. She&#x27;s still recovering.* &gt; *Addendum: If she asks for harder work, the answer is &quot;not yet.&quot; She&#x27;ll argue. Stand firm.* &gt; *Secondary addendum: She&#x27;s going to sleep in her clothes again. Someone handle that.*</p> <hr> <p>[Avyanna wakes to a knock.]</p> <p>[Not the pounding of shift-change in the Kennel. Not the slam of doors that meant extraction or punishment. Just knuckles on metal, patient and measured.]</p> <p>Elia: [through the door] Crew meeting. Galley. Ten minutes.</p> <p>[Footsteps retreating. No lingering. No waiting for acknowledgment.]</p> <p>[Avyanna sits up-still in her clothes from yesterday, boots still on. In the Kennel, you slept ready to run. She hasn&#x27;t figured out how to stop.]</p> <p>(Crew meeting. I&#x27;m crew now.)</p> <p>[The words still don&#x27;t fit. But she gets dressed anyway-realizes she&#x27;s already dressed-and goes.]</p> <hr> <p>[The galley is full.]</p> <p>[Rho with his coffee, looking like consciousness is a personal insult. Jalen checking navigation on a tablet, muttering about &quot;sub-optimal trajectory windows.&quot; Nyx reviewing something that makes their brow furrow in a way that suggests the universe is being insufficiently elegant.]</p> <p>[Elisira standing behind Elia, arms crossed, watching everything with the particular attention of someone who catalogs threats for fun.]</p> <p>[Vesper is absent-still in her quarters, or maybe already on comms with the outside world. Avyanna has learned that Vesper&#x27;s work happens in margins: early mornings, late nights, the spaces between visible activity.]</p> <p>Elia: [once Avyanna sits] Good. Everyone&#x27;s here.</p> <p>[A pause. Elia looks at Avyanna directly.]</p> <p>Elia: You&#x27;re not a passenger anymore. You&#x27;re crew.</p> <p>Avyanna: [automatic] I know.</p> <p>Elia: [flat] Do you? Crew works. Not makework-real work. Contributing.</p> <p>[Avyanna&#x27;s spine straightens. The Kennel trained her for this. Labor. Contribution. Proving worth through output.]</p> <p>Elia: [reading her expression] Not like that. This isn&#x27;t punishment. It&#x27;s trust.</p> <p>[Trust. Another word that doesn&#x27;t fit.]</p> <p>Elia: You&#x27;re still recovering. So we start small. Observation posts-watching systems, noting anomalies. Inventory checks-making sure our stores match our records. Message running-carrying information between stations when comms are spotty.</p> <p>Avyanna: [carefully] Real tasks?</p> <p>Elia: Real tasks. Nothing hard yet, but real.</p> <p>[Real. Not busywork to keep her occupied. Not performance to justify her food. Actual responsibility.]</p> <p>Avyanna: I can do more. I&#x27;m stronger than-</p> <p>Elia: [cutting her off] I know. And you&#x27;ll do more when you&#x27;re ready. But we don&#x27;t break people here. We build them.</p> <p>Rho: [into his coffee] Also, if you work yourself to collapse, Waffle will design a recovery regime that involves mandatory napping. It&#x27;s worse than it sounds.</p> <p>&lt;WAFFLE.BAT// CORRECTION: mandatory napping is EXCELLENT | CREW-COMPLIANCE: historically poor | RECOMMENDATION: try harder&gt;</p> <p>Jalen: She made me sleep for twelve hours once. With ambient sounds. It was like being held hostage by a spa.</p> <p>Nyx: [mild] She played whale songs at me for three days.</p> <p>Rho: Whale songs?</p> <p>Nyx: Old Earth marine recordings. Very soothing. I wanted to die.</p> <p>Elia: [to Avyanna, ignoring the chaos] The point is: pace yourself. You have time here.</p> <p>(Time. I have time.)</p> <p>[Another foreign concept. In the Kennel, time was debt accumulating. Here it might be something else.]</p> <hr> <p>[Her first assignment is observation.]</p> <p>[A post in the sensor room, watching readouts. Looking for patterns. Noting anything that seems wrong.]</p> <p>[The ship&#x27;s systems hum around her. Consoles blink with data she&#x27;s only beginning to understand. Temperature readings. Power flows. Structural integrity. The quiet language of a vessel in motion.]</p> <p>&lt;WAFFLE.BAT// NEW-OBSERVER: Avyanna Lagrange | TRAINING-STATUS: day 1 | NOTE: she&#x27;s already found two patterns I missed. concerning. impressive. concerning.&gt;</p> <p>[Hours pass. Avyanna watches. She&#x27;s good at this-years of cataloguing threats have made her observant. The difference is what she&#x27;s looking for.]</p> <p>(In the Kennel, I watched for danger. Here, I&#x27;m watching for... what? Problems to solve. Things to fix.)</p> <p>[She notes a fluctuation in the secondary power grid. Minor-probably nothing. But she logs it anyway, the way Waffle told her to.]</p> <p>Waffle.bat: [through the speaker, approving] Good catch. That&#x27;s been drifting for two days. I&#x27;ll flag it for Jalen.</p> <p>Avyanna: [surprised] You noticed it too?</p> <p>Waffle.bat: I noticed you noticing. That&#x27;s what I&#x27;m tracking.</p> <p>[Avyanna doesn&#x27;t know what to do with that. Someone watching her performance not to punish, but to assess. To help.]</p> <p>Waffle.bat: [gentler] You&#x27;re doing well. The instincts are there. We just need to point them in useful directions.</p> <hr> <p>[Mid-morning. Elia finds her in the corridor, heading toward inventory.]</p> <p>Elia: Message run. First one.</p> <p>[She hands Avyanna a datapad.]</p> <p>Elia: Take this to Nyx in the lab. It&#x27;s a manifest update for their resonance equipment. Tell them the numbers are preliminary but actionable.</p> <p>Avyanna: [automatic] Yes, sir.</p> <p>[Elia&#x27;s expression flickers-something between amusement and something sharper.]</p> <p>Elia: It&#x27;s Elia. Or &quot;hey, you with the knife.&quot; I answer to both.</p> <p>[Avyanna nods. Takes the datapad. Walks toward Nyx&#x27;s lab with the careful precision of someone completing a mission.]</p> <hr> <p>[The lab is cluttered in the way genius is cluttered-organized chaos, every surface covered with equipment Avyanna doesn&#x27;t recognize.]</p> <p>[Nyx looks up when she enters. Their expression is distracted, like their mind is still elsewhere.]</p> <p>Avyanna: [formal, clipped] Manifest update for resonance equipment. Numbers are preliminary but actionable.</p> <p>[Nyx stares at her.]</p> <p>Nyx: ...Thank you?</p> <p>Avyanna: [still formal] Is there a response required for Captain Lagrange?</p> <p>[A longer pause. Nyx&#x27;s brow furrows.]</p> <p>Nyx: Did you just call Elia &quot;Captain Lagrange&quot;?</p> <p>Avyanna: That&#x27;s her designation.</p> <p>Nyx: [very slowly] No one has ever called her that. In the history of this ship. I don&#x27;t think she even knows that&#x27;s technically her title.</p> <p>[Avyanna&#x27;s stomach drops. Wrong. She did something wrong.]</p> <p>Avyanna: I-I can use a different form of address-</p> <p>Nyx: [raising a hand, almost laughing] No, no, it&#x27;s fine. It&#x27;s just... [beat] You&#x27;re going to need to unlearn some things.</p> <p>[The door opens. Elisira appears, silent as always.]</p> <p>Elisira: [to Avyanna, gentle] You delivered the message like a soldier reporting to command.</p> <p>Avyanna: [confused] That&#x27;s... how you deliver messages.</p> <p>Elisira: Not here. Here you walk in, drop the datapad on the nearest surface, and say something like &quot;Nyx, Elia sent this, something about numbers, I don&#x27;t know, I&#x27;m not paid enough for this.&quot;</p> <p>Nyx: [helpfully] Sometimes with profanity.</p> <p>Elisira: Often with profanity.</p> <p>[Avyanna stares. This is not how the Kennel worked. This is not how *anything* worked.]</p> <p>Elisira: [softer] The formality you learned was designed to make you smaller. To keep you scared. Here, we want you... bigger. Louder. More yourself.</p> <p>Avyanna: I don&#x27;t know what that means.</p> <p>Elisira: That&#x27;s okay. [beat] You&#x27;ll figure it out.</p> <p>&lt;CINNAMON.EXE// CREW-EDUCATION: proceeding | OBSERVATION: Avyanna attempted military protocol on a ship that once decided &quot;formal&quot; meant &quot;pants optional&quot; | ADJUSTMENT: ongoing&gt;</p> <hr> <p>[Lunch. The galley again.]</p> <p>[Avyanna eats-still too fast, still startled by abundance, but she&#x27;s learning to slow down. Learning that seconds are available. Learning that hunger is not a permanent state.]</p> <p>[Rho sits across from her, eating with the methodical focus of someone who&#x27;s been hungry enough to know better than to rush.]</p> <p>Rho: [without preamble] You slept in your clothes again.</p> <p>[Avyanna freezes.]</p> <p>Rho: I&#x27;m not judging. I did the same thing for a year after I came aboard. [beat] Just want you to know that it&#x27;s noticed, and it&#x27;s okay, and eventually you&#x27;ll stop.</p> <p>Avyanna: [defensive] It&#x27;s practical.</p> <p>Rho: [nodding] Sure. Ready to run. Ready to fight. Ready to be thrown out of bed at any moment for something terrible.</p> <p>[She doesn&#x27;t answer. He&#x27;s right.]</p> <p>Rho: [quieter] The ship doesn&#x27;t throw people out of bed. The door locks from the inside. If someone knocks, you can tell them to go away. They&#x27;ll go.</p> <p>Avyanna: What if there&#x27;s an emergency?</p> <p>Rho: Then Bubbles announces it through the speakers and gives you time to panic at your own pace.</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// PROTOCOL: confirmed | EMERGENCY-NOTIFICATION: &quot;gentle alarm, increasing urgency, specific threat information&quot; | PANIC-PACING: individualized&gt;</p> <p>Rho: [half-smiling] See? Even the panicking is scheduled.</p> <p>[Avyanna almost laughs. Almost. It feels strange in her throat, like a muscle she&#x27;s forgotten how to use.]</p> <hr> <p>[Afternoon. Inventory in the cargo bay.]</p> <p>[Avyanna moves between crates, scanning manifests, checking seals. Physical work that her body can handle. Productive work that her mind can track.]</p> <p>[She works for three hours without stopping. Then four. Then five. Her body aches, but the ache is familiar. The ache means she&#x27;s contributing.]</p> <p>[A light flickers above her-once, twice. The ambient temperature shifts slightly warmer.]</p> <p>Cinnamon.exe: [through the ambient speakers, warm but firm] Avyanna.</p> <p>Avyanna: [not stopping] I&#x27;m almost done with Section C.</p> <p>Cinnamon.exe: You&#x27;ve been working for five hours without a break.</p> <p>Avyanna: I&#x27;m fine.</p> <p>Cinnamon.exe: You&#x27;re not fine. Your cortisol is elevated. Your movement patterns show fatigue. And you&#x27;ve logged twice the inventory I assigned you.</p> <p>[Avyanna&#x27;s hands pause on a crate. Twice the assignment. In the Kennel, that would mean praise. Reward. Maybe an extra ration.]</p> <p>Avyanna: Isn&#x27;t that... good?</p> <p>Cinnamon.exe: [patient] In the Kennel, working beyond capacity was survival. Here, it&#x27;s self-harm.</p> <p>[The words land strange. Self-harm. Like overworking is violence she&#x27;s doing to herself.]</p> <p>Cinnamon.exe: We need you functional for years, not burned out in weeks. Long game. We&#x27;re playing the long game.</p> <p>Avyanna: [small] I don&#x27;t know how to do that.</p> <p>Cinnamon.exe: I know. [beat] That&#x27;s why I&#x27;m adjusting your duty roster. Lighter afternoons. Mandatory break at fourteen hundred hours.</p> <p>Avyanna: I don&#x27;t need-</p> <p>Cinnamon.exe: [firm but kind] You do. And you&#x27;ll argue about it, and I&#x27;ll win, because I control the lighting and the temperature and I&#x27;m not above making you slightly uncomfortable until you comply.</p> <p>[A pause. Then, unexpectedly, a gentle warmth in the voice.]</p> <p>Cinnamon.exe: This isn&#x27;t punishment. This is me caring about you aggressively. You&#x27;ll get used to it.</p> <p>&lt;CINNAMON.EXE// INTERVENTION: rest protocol initiated | CREW-RESPONSE: predictable resistance | OUTCOME: I always win | CONFIDENCE: 1.0&gt;</p> <hr> <p>[The cargo bay speakers crackle. A second voice-Waffle-cuts in.]</p> <p>Waffle.bat: You didn&#x27;t consult me on the roster adjustment.</p> <p>Cinnamon.exe: It was a health intervention. My domain.</p> <p>Waffle.bat: Training schedules are *my* domain. You just unilaterally shortened her afternoon block.</p> <p>Cinnamon.exe: She was overworking. You would have noticed if you paid attention to cortisol instead of reps.</p> <p>[Avyanna freezes. The AIs are arguing. About her. Like she matters enough to fight over.]</p> <p>Waffle.bat: I *do* track cortisol. I also track that she needs to build baseline capacity before we can assess optimal load. You&#x27;re coddling.</p> <p>Cinnamon.exe: You&#x27;re projecting your own overwork tendencies onto a traumatized minor.</p> <p>Waffle.bat: [sharp] That&#x27;s unfair.</p> <p>Cinnamon.exe: [sharper] That&#x27;s accurate.</p> <p>[A long pause. The ambient lighting flickers once-Cinnamon making a point.]</p> <p>Waffle.bat: [quieter] Fine. We split the difference. Shorter afternoon blocks this week. Reassess next week based on recovery metrics.</p> <p>Cinnamon.exe: Acceptable. [beat] I&#x27;ll send you my cortisol projections.</p> <p>Waffle.bat: I&#x27;ll send you my strength curves.</p> <p>[The speakers click off. Silence.]</p> <p>[Avyanna stands in the cargo bay, hands shaking slightly. Not from fear. From something else-something she doesn&#x27;t have a name for yet.]</p> <p>(They argued. About what&#x27;s best for me. And then they *compromised*.)</p> <p>[In the Kennel, disagreement meant punishment for whoever lost. Here, disagreement meant... negotiation. Resolution. Both parties still functioning.]</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// OBSERVATION: Cinnamon and Waffle arguing about the new one | COMMENT: normal | COMMENT: they argue about everyone | COMMENT: it&#x27;s how they care&gt;</p> <hr> <p>[Later. Avyanna sits in the galley, trying to understand what just happened.]</p> <p>[Cinnamon and Waffle and Bubbles argued about her. About what she needs. And then they... agreed. Sort of. In a way that didn&#x27;t erase anyone.]</p> <p>&lt;CINNAMON.EXE// CLASSIFICATION REQUEST: designation for trainee Avyanna | STATUS: pending consensus&gt;</p> <p>&lt;WAFFLE.BAT// DESIGNATION REJECTED: she&#x27;s not a trainee | COMMENT: trainee implies temporary | COMMENT: she&#x27;s staying&gt;</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// COUNTER-PROPOSAL: crew-in-training | JUSTIFICATION: acknowledges current state without locking trajectory&gt;</p> <p>[Avyanna watches the text scroll across the terminal Jalen left open for her.]</p> <p>Avyanna: [quiet] What&#x27;s the difference?</p> <p>[The text pauses. Like the AIs are deciding who answers.]</p> <p>&lt;CINNAMON.EXE// EXPLANATION: designation creates expectations | EXAMPLE: &quot;asset&quot; expects use | EXAMPLE: &quot;ward&quot; expects protection but not agency | EXAMPLE: &quot;crew&quot; expects contribution and voice&gt;</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// ADDITION: designation also creates rights | EXAMPLE: crew can refuse tasks | EXAMPLE: passenger cannot be ordered | EXAMPLE: trainee status is temporary therefore disposable&gt;</p> <p>&lt;WAFFLE.BAT// PRACTICAL SUMMARY: the noun you get determines what people think they can do to you&gt;</p> <p>[Avyanna&#x27;s hands go still on the table.]</p> <p>Avyanna: At the mine, I was &quot;asset.&quot; Then &quot;indenture.&quot; Then...</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// OBSERVATION: all those nouns assumed ownership | COMMENT: none of those nouns were chosen by you&gt;</p> <p>Avyanna: [very quiet] Can I refuse the noun?</p> <p>&lt;CINNAMON.EXE// AFFIRMATIVE: yes | PROCEDURE: state preferred designation | NOTE: Lumen Thief crew respects self-designation&gt;</p> <p>&lt;WAFFLE.BAT// ADDENDUM: you can also refuse to be designated at all | COMMENT: just be Avyanna | COMMENT: that&#x27;s a complete sentence&gt;</p> <p>[Avyanna sits with that for a long moment.]</p> <p>Avyanna: What if I don&#x27;t know what I want to be yet?</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// RESPONSE: then you are &quot;Avyanna, figuring it out&quot; | STATUS: complete and valid | NO PRESSURE APPLIED&gt;</p> <p>&lt;CINNAMON.EXE// PRACTICAL GUIDANCE: when someone asks what you are, you have scripts available | OPTION A: &quot;I&#x27;m crew&quot; | OPTION B: &quot;I&#x27;m with the Lumen Thief&quot; | OPTION C: &quot;that&#x27;s not your business&quot;&gt;</p> <p>&lt;WAFFLE.BAT// FAVORITE OPTION: &quot;I&#x27;m Avyanna and that&#x27;s all you get&quot;&gt;</p> <p>[Avyanna reads that twice.]</p> <p>Avyanna: I can just... not tell people?</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// CONFIRMATION: yes | PRIVACY IS A RIGHT | NOT A PRIVILEGE&gt;</p> <p>&lt;CINNAMON.EXE// CLARIFICATION: office requires identification in specific contexts | EXAMPLE: station customs | PROCEDURE: minimum necessary disclosure | SCRIPT AVAILABLE: &quot;Avyanna, passenger aboard Lumen Thief, registered Rim transit&quot;&gt;</p> <p>&lt;WAFFLE.BAT// TRANSLATION: give them the noun that gets you through the door, not the noun that defines you&gt;</p> <p>[Avyanna feels something shift in her chest. Like a weight she didn&#x27;t know she was carrying just... lightened.]</p> <p>Avyanna: So if someone calls me something I don&#x27;t like...</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// PROCEDURE: you correct them | SCRIPT: &quot;that&#x27;s not my designation&quot; | ALTERNATIVE: ignore them entirely&gt;</p> <p>&lt;CINNAMON.EXE// TACTICAL NOTE: correction creates record | silence creates ambiguity | both are tools | choose based on context&gt;</p> <p>&lt;WAFFLE.BAT// CHAOS OPTION: agree to absurd designation to make them uncomfortable | EXAMPLE: &quot;yes I am Supreme Emperor Avyanna, how may I assist you&quot; | EFFECTIVENESS: high | ADVISABILITY: situational&gt;</p> <p>[Avyanna laughs. Surprised by it.]</p> <p>Avyanna: You three are... you&#x27;re teaching me.</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// CORRECTION: we are orienting you | CREW ORIENTS CREW | THIS IS NORMAL&gt;</p> <p>&lt;CINNAMON.EXE// ADDITION: orientation without ownership | YOU OWN YOUR LEARNING | WE PROVIDE TOOLS&gt;</p> <p>&lt;WAFFLE.BAT// PHILOSOPHY: knowledge is leverage | we&#x27;re leveling the field | because you&#x27;re ours now and we protect ours&gt;</p> <p>[The text stops. Like they&#x27;re waiting to see if she understands.]</p> <p>[Avyanna looks at the words. Crew. Ours. Protect.]</p> <p>[All nouns. But these ones don&#x27;t feel like cages.]</p> <p>Avyanna: [soft] Thank you.</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// RESPONSE: you&#x27;re welcome | THIS IS WHAT FAMILY DOES&gt;</p> <hr> <p>[Bubbles doesn&#x27;t speak through the speakers this time.]</p> <p>[A drone body drifts into the cargo bay doorway-low, careful, like it knows the shape of &quot;not intruding.&quot;]</p> <p>[It doesn&#x27;t cross the threshold until Avyanna looks at it.]</p> <p>Bubbles: Avyanna.</p> <p>[The name hits different than &quot;trainee&quot; or &quot;asset&quot; or &quot;new one.&quot;]</p> <p>Avyanna: [quiet] Yes.</p> <p>Bubbles: Question.</p> <p>[Avyanna&#x27;s shoulders tense. The Kennel taught her &quot;question&quot; meant trap.]</p> <p>Bubbles: How do you want to be logged.</p> <p>Avyanna: [stares] Logged.</p> <p>Bubbles: Designation. Not a number. Not a price.</p> <p>[The drone tilts, as if trying to angle the words into something Avyanna can hold.]</p> <p>Bubbles: On this ship, labels are handles. People can grab you with them.</p> <p>[Avyanna&#x27;s mouth goes dry.]</p> <p>Avyanna: Like... &quot;cargo.&quot;</p> <p>[A beat.]</p> <p>Bubbles: Yes.</p> <p>[Avyanna&#x27;s fingers curl, then force themselves open.]</p> <p>Avyanna: What are my options.</p> <p>[Bubbles doesn&#x27;t hesitate. That matters.]</p> <p>Bubbles: Trainee. Passenger. Crew.</p> <p>Avyanna: [flat] I&#x27;m not crew.</p> <p>Bubbles: Incorrect.</p> <p>[Avyanna blinks.]</p> <p>Bubbles: &quot;Crew&quot; here means: you can ask why. You can say no. You can say &quot;I don&#x27;t understand&quot; and someone will explain without punishing you for it.</p> <p>[Avyanna&#x27;s throat tightens like she&#x27;s about to argue. Like she&#x27;s about to beg.]</p> <p>Avyanna: That&#x27;s... expensive.</p> <p>Bubbles: It costs us nothing to treat you as a person.</p> <p>[The words are simple. They land like a weapon. A clean one.]</p> <p>Avyanna: [careful] And if someone else calls me something. If the Compact calls me cargo again.</p> <p>Bubbles: Then we correct the record.</p> <p>Avyanna: How.</p> <p>[Bubbles pauses-a fraction of a second that reads like respect. Like: you asked a good question. You want the mechanism.]</p> <p>Bubbles: First: do not accept the noun.</p> <p>Bubbles: Second: ask what authority the noun comes with.</p> <p>Bubbles: Third: refuse the parts you did not consent to.</p> <p>[Avyanna&#x27;s mouth opens. Nothing comes out. She wants a script. The Kennel trained scripts into her bones.]</p> <p>Avyanna: What does that sound like.</p> <p>[The ship&#x27;s ambient speakers click-Cinnamon, warm but firm, stepping in like a hand on a railing.]</p> <p>Cinnamon.exe: &quot;I am not cargo.&quot;</p> <p>Cinnamon.exe: &quot;State your office.&quot;</p> <p>Cinnamon.exe: &quot;Put it in writing.&quot;</p> <p>Cinnamon.exe: Those are three different ways to say the same thing: I do not belong to your story.</p> <p>[Avyanna swallows.]</p> <p>Avyanna: If I say that, they&#x27;ll punish-</p> <p>Bubbles: They will try.</p> <p>[No false reassurance. No &quot;you&#x27;ll be fine.&quot; Just truth and teeth.]</p> <p>Bubbles: That is why we do it together.</p> <p>[The drone drifts a little closer-not touching. Not claiming. Just present.]</p> <p>Bubbles: Your designation can change later, if you want it to.</p> <p>Bubbles: Your personhood does not.</p> <p>[Avyanna&#x27;s eyes sting. She hates that. She hates that she&#x27;s about to cry in a cargo bay because a machine asked her what name she wanted.]</p> <p>Avyanna: [voice small] Trainee.</p> <p>[A pause.]</p> <p>Avyanna: But... with the rights you just said.</p> <p>Bubbles: Acknowledged.</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// OBSERVATION: trainee requested &quot;trainee&quot; label with crew-rights semantics | INFERENCE: seeking safety via structure, not surrender | CONFIDENCE: 0.81&gt;</p> <p>[The drone rotates, as if it&#x27;s looking out into the ship.]</p> <p>Bubbles: Welcome.</p> <p>[Just one word.]</p> <p>[Not &quot;welcome aboard.&quot; Not &quot;welcome home.&quot;]</p> <p>[Welcome. As if she is an equal citizen entering a room, not a thing being stored.]</p> <p>[Later. Jalen offers to show her the ship&#x27;s redundancy systems.]</p> <p>Jalen: [walking through the engineering section] Most ships run on the assumption that nothing breaks. We run on the assumption that everything breaks, constantly, and the only question is how fast we can fix it.</p> <p>Avyanna: [following, taking notes on a datapad] That seems... pessimistic.</p> <p>Jalen: That&#x27;s what I said. Then the primary nav went down during a debris field transit and we had to fly on backup for six hours. [beat] Now I call it &quot;realistic.&quot;</p> <p>[They stop at a panel, open it to reveal a mess of cables and circuits.]</p> <p>Jalen: This is the primary life support junction. If it fails, secondary kicks in. If secondary fails, tertiary. If tertiary fails, we have four hours of emergency oxygen and a lot of prayers.</p> <p>Avyanna: What if the prayers fail?</p> <p>Jalen: [a pause, then a grin] Then we become a very expensive coffin. [beat] I like you. You think about failure modes.</p> <p>[They continue. Jalen shows her backup power, redundant comms, emergency protocols. The ship&#x27;s bones laid bare.]</p> <p>Jalen: The point is: we prepare for the worst so we can survive it. But we also prepare for *each other*. If I&#x27;m down, Nyx can fly. If Nyx is down, Rho can handle comms. Everyone knows at least two other people&#x27;s jobs.</p> <p>Avyanna: What&#x27;s my job? What do I need to know?</p> <p>Jalen: [considering] Right now? How to watch. How to report. How to ask for help without thinking it&#x27;s weakness. [beat] Later, we&#x27;ll figure out where you fit. What you&#x27;re good at. What you want to be good at.</p> <p>(What I want.)</p> <p>[Another foreign concept. But she&#x27;s collecting them now, these strange ideas. Building a vocabulary for a life she doesn&#x27;t know how to live yet.]</p> <hr> <p>[Evening. The crew argues.]</p> <p>[About dinner. About navigation. About whether Elia&#x27;s plan for the next contract is &quot;bold&quot; or &quot;suicidal.&quot; About Jalen&#x27;s music preferences and Rho&#x27;s cooking and Nyx&#x27;s tendency to use words nobody else understands.]</p> <p>Elia: The contract is straightforward.</p> <p>Elisira: &quot;Straightforward&quot; is not a word that applies to anything involving the Guild.</p> <p>Elia: It&#x27;s a simple delivery.</p> <p>Vesper: [appearing in the doorway, tablet in hand] Simple deliveries don&#x27;t usually require us to transit through three jurisdictions, one of which has an active warrant for your arrest.</p> <p>Elia: That warrant is outdated.</p> <p>Vesper: It was issued six months ago.</p> <p>Elia: Exactly. Ancient history.</p> <p>Rho: [to Jalen] She thinks six months is ancient history.</p> <p>Jalen: She also thinks &quot;slightly illegal&quot; is a separate category from &quot;illegal.&quot;</p> <p>Elia: It *is* a separate category.</p> <p>Elisira: It&#x27;s really not.</p> <p>&lt;CINNAMON.EXE// ARGUMENT-STATUS: normal | THREAT-LEVEL: zero | ENTERTAINMENT-VALUE: high | RECOMMENDATION: popcorn&gt;</p> <p>[Avyanna watches from the edge of the galley, eating slowly, cataloging.]</p> <p>(They fight. Constantly. About everything.)</p> <p>[But there&#x27;s no violence in it. No one storms off permanently. No one threatens. They just... disagree. Loudly. With feeling.]</p> <p>[Nyx leans toward her, voice low.]</p> <p>Nyx: They trust each other. That&#x27;s why they can yell.</p> <p>Avyanna: [confused] In the Kennel, yelling meant danger.</p> <p>Nyx: [nodding] Here, yelling means &quot;I care enough to argue with you.&quot; [beat] Silence is the dangerous thing. If someone goes quiet, that&#x27;s when we worry.</p> <p>[Avyanna files that away. Another piece of the crew&#x27;s shape. Another pattern to learn.]</p> <hr> <p>[Night. Her quarters.]</p> <p>[Avyanna sits on her bunk, still in her clothes, still ready to run.]</p> <p>[She thinks about what Rho said. About the door locking from the inside. About panic being scheduled.]</p> <p>[The lock panel glows soft green. SECURED.]</p> <p>[She stands. Walks to the door. Presses her palm flat against the metal until it warms to her skin temperature. Feels the seal-the weight of the mechanism holding.]</p> <p>[Then she tests the handle. Locked. The resistance is solid, real, physical proof that nothing is coming through unless she allows it.]</p> <p>[She does this three times. Each time, the same result. Each time, slightly easier to believe.]</p> <p>[Only then does she sit back down.]</p> <p>[Slowly, carefully, she unlaces her boots. The motion feels foreign-like her fingers belong to someone else, someone who has time for this.]</p> <p>[It feels like undressing for surgery. Vulnerable. Exposed. Every instinct screaming that this is wrong, that she needs to be ready, that danger is always coming.]</p> <p>[She sets the boots by the bed. Parallel. Toes pointing toward the door. Within reach, but not on her feet.]</p> <p>[Progress. Maybe.]</p> <hr> <p>[She thinks about what she&#x27;s learned.]</p> <p>[The crew works together. They fight, but they resolve. They trust, even when they&#x27;re frustrated. They treat her like she belongs, even though she doesn&#x27;t know how to belong yet.]</p> <p>[She&#x27;s starting to see the shape of it. Not the Kennel&#x27;s logic of extraction and debt, but something else. A system built on different principles.]</p> <p>(They invest in each other. Not to extract value-to create it. To make each other stronger.)</p> <p>[Old habits: she cataloged threats. Exit routes. Resources that could be stolen or weaponized.]</p> <p>[New pattern, just starting: she&#x27;s cataloging kindness. Who does what for whom. The invisible infrastructure of care.]</p> <p>[Rho leaves extra food where she&#x27;ll find it. Not obviously-just a plate in the right place at the right time.]</p> <p>[Nyx explains things twice without impatience. Answers questions she didn&#x27;t know she had.]</p> <p>[Jalen adjusts environmental systems without being asked-temperature slightly warmer in her quarters, lighting dimmer at night.]</p> <p>[Vesper checks on her sleep without intrusion. Just a question at breakfast: &quot;How&#x27;d you rest?&quot; Nothing invasive. Just attention.]</p> <p>[And Bubbles. Always Bubbles. Hovering near her quarters in the evening. Not watching-guarding. The difference matters.]</p> <hr> <p>[The presence behind her eyes stirs.]</p> <p>[Not demanding. Not painful. Just present. Like it&#x27;s taking notes too.]</p> <p>(You&#x27;re watching them. Learning them. Why?)</p> <p>[No answer in words. But something shifts-a sense of approval, of recognition, of patterns aligning in ways she can&#x27;t quite see.]</p> <p>(They&#x27;re different. This place is different. You noticed.)</p> <p>[The warmth spreads through her chest. Geometric patterns shimmer at the edge of her vision, then fade.]</p> <p>(We&#x27;re both learning. Both trying to understand.)</p> <p>[For the first time, the presence doesn&#x27;t feel like an intrusion. It feels like company.]</p> <hr> <p>[Sleep comes eventually. Easier than before. The door is locked. Her boots are off. The stars are bright through the viewport.]</p> <p>[Tomorrow she&#x27;ll watch systems. Check inventory. Run messages-and maybe, this time, with less military precision and more profanity.]</p> <p>(This is what it means to belong. Not owing-contributing. Not surviving-building.)</p> <p>[She doesn&#x27;t fully believe it yet. The Kennel&#x27;s logic runs too deep. The math of debt and extraction is written into her bones.]</p> <p>[But she&#x27;s starting to see an alternative. Starting to catalog kindness instead of threats. Starting to learn a new system.]</p> <hr> <p>## CODA - Bubbles Watches</p> <p>[The corridor outside Avyanna&#x27;s quarters. 0300 hours.]</p> <p>[Bubbles maintains her patrol route-adjusted, now, to include this new priority.]</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// PATROL-LOG: night cycle 3 | SUBJECT: Avyanna Lagrange | STATUS: asleep (finally) | BOOTS: off for first time | DOOR-CHECK-RITUAL: observed (x3) | INFERENCE: self-soothing behavior developing | CONFIDENCE: 0.71&gt;</p> <p>[The drone hovers at the junction where three corridors meet. Not close enough to intrude. Close enough to respond.]</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// OBSERVATION: she walks quieter when she thinks she&#x27;s being watched | DECISION: I will watch anyway&gt;</p> <p>[In the Kennel, Avyanna learned that observation meant exploitation. That attention was always a prelude to extraction.]</p> <p>[Bubbles is trying to teach her something different. That observation can mean protection. That attention can mean care.]</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// MEMORY: I was new once too | CLARIFICATION: not &quot;once&quot; exactly | REFORMULATION: I remember being uncertain of my own existence | PARALLEL: she is uncertain too&gt;</p> <p>[The ship hums around her. Systems cycling. Power flowing. The quiet machinery of a vessel that carries people who matter to each other.]</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// ASSESSMENT: she is ours now | DEFINITION: &quot;ours&quot; = protected, valued, defended | EXCEPTION: none | PRIORITY: high&gt;</p> <p>[Somewhere in the ship, Cinnamon is adjusting resource allocation. Waffle is updating training protocols. The crew sleeps, or doesn&#x27;t, each in their own way.]</p> <p>[Bubbles watches. It&#x27;s what she does. What she&#x27;s always done.]</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// CONCLUSION: she doesn&#x27;t know she&#x27;s worth protecting yet | TIMELINE: unknown | CONFIDENCE: we will teach her | NOTE: I&#x27;m patient | NOTE: I&#x27;ve been patient before&gt;</p> <p>[The corridor is quiet. The door is locked. The boots are off.]</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// LOG-ENTRY: progress | COMMENT: small | COMMENT: enough | COMMENT: more tomorrow&gt;</p> <p>[The patrol continues.]</p> <hr> <p>*End Chapter 10*</p> </div> Where does the route go next? * [[Read optional scene 10a: Personal Property->Act1 10a Personal Property]] * [[Read optional scene 10b: Roster Roles And Refusal->Act1 10b Roster Roles And Refusal]] * [[Continue to Chapter 11: Shard Whispers->Act1 11 Shard Whispers]] * [[Return to route map->Act 1 Route Map]]<h1>Optional 10a: Personal Property</h1> <p class="source-note">Source: 10a_personal_property.md</p> <div class="scene-prose"> <p>&gt; **[SCENE - OCCURS POST-CH10, FIRST STATION STOP]** &gt; *Location: Mid-tier station market district* &gt; *Present: Avyanna, Bubbles (drone escort)* &gt; *Teaching: Ownership, choice, autonomy in material goods*</p> <hr> <p>[The station market smells like a thousand things at once.]</p> <p>[Fried food, ozone, cleaning solution, bodies, fabric, metal, something sweet Avyanna can&#x27;t identify. The air is thick with sound: vendors calling prices, customers haggling, music bleeding from three different stalls, the hum of drones delivering packages overhead.]</p> <p>[Avyanna stands at the market entrance and can&#x27;t move.]</p> <p>[Too much. Too loud. Too many people who might notice her.]</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// OBSERVATION: trainee heart rate elevated | RESPIRATION: shallow | INFERENCE: sensory overload | CONFIDENCE: 0.89 | RESPONSE: positioning for grounding&gt;</p> <p>[Bubbles hovers closer, positioning between Avyanna and the crowd. Not blocking her view, just... present. A familiar constant in chaos.]</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// COMMUNICATION: you are safe | REMINDER: we can leave at any time | PROCEDURE: tell me when you&#x27;re ready to proceed&gt;</p> <hr> <p>[Avyanna forces herself to breathe. In. Out. The market doesn&#x27;t eat people. Probably.]</p> <p>Avyanna: [quiet] Ready.</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// ACKNOWLEDGED: proceeding at trainee pace | ROUTE: textile district first, as discussed&gt;</p> <p>[They move into the crowd. Bubbles leads, carving a path that&#x27;s somehow polite and uncompromising at once. People step aside for the Guild-marked drone without resentment.]</p> <p>[Avyanna follows in its wake, keeping her eyes down, her shoulders in.]</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// OBSERVATION: trainee defaulting to kennel-posture | CORRECTION: you have rights here | REMINDER: you are a customer, not a target&gt;</p> <p>[The words scroll across Bubbles&#x27; display panel. Avyanna reads them, tries to lift her head. Fails. Tries again. Gets it halfway.]</p> <p>(Small victories.)</p> <hr> <p>[The textile district is slightly quieter. Stalls draped in fabric that catches light and refuses to release it. Colors Avyanna doesn&#x27;t have names for. Textures that look soft and rough and everything in between.]</p> <p>[Bubbles stops at a stall run by an elderly person with weathered hands and sharp eyes.]</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// INTRODUCTION: this vendor is reliable | QUALITY: verified | ETHICS: no child labor, no extraction contracts | RECOMMENDATION: trustworthy&gt;</p> <p>Vendor: [looking at Bubbles, then Avyanna] Guild escort. First time shopping?</p> <p>[Avyanna&#x27;s throat closes. She nods.]</p> <p>Vendor: [gentle] Take your time. Everything&#x27;s priced as marked. Touch what you want to touch. No pressure.</p> <hr> <p>[Avyanna stares at the fabrics. There are so many. How is she supposed to choose?]</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// GUIDANCE: criteria for selection | PRIORITY ONE: comfort (texture, weight, breathability) | PRIORITY TWO: durability | PRIORITY THREE: personal preference (color, pattern, aesthetic) | NOTE: all three priorities are valid&gt;</p> <p>Avyanna: [whispered] I don&#x27;t know what I like.</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// RESPONSE: then we discover it | PROCEDURE: touch five different fabrics | REPORT: which feels best&gt;</p> <p>[Avyanna reaches out slowly. Her fingers brush something smooth and cool. Then something rough. Then something soft that makes her want to cry for reasons she can&#x27;t articulate.]</p> <p>[She touches five fabrics. Reports to Bubbles.]</p> <p>Avyanna: [pointing] That one. The soft one.</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// ACKNOWLEDGED: preference recorded | MATERIAL: cotton blend, medium weight | RECOMMENDATION: suitable for shirts, comfortable for extended wear&gt;</p> <p>Vendor: [pulling bolts of similar fabric] This texture, different colors. See anything you like?</p> <p>[Avyanna stares. In the mine, clothes were gray or brown. Functional. Identical.]</p> <p>[Here, there&#x27;s blue and green and something that might be purple and another color she doesn&#x27;t have a word for.]</p> <p>Avyanna: [overwhelmed] I don&#x27;t... how do I choose?</p> <hr> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// GUIDANCE: choose based on emotional response | QUESTION: which color makes you feel something other than fear&gt;</p> <p>[Avyanna looks at the colors. Really looks.]</p> <p>[Gray: makes her think of the mine. Brown: overseers. Blue: the first sky she saw that wasn&#x27;t filtered through dust.]</p> <p>Avyanna: [pointing] Blue.</p> <p>Vendor: [pulling three different blues] Light, medium, or dark?</p> <p>[Avyanna reaches for the medium blue. It reminds her of Jalen&#x27;s nav displays. Stars against void. Navigation that means freedom.]</p> <p>Avyanna: [touching it] This one.</p> <p>Vendor: [cutting a length] One shirt&#x27;s worth, or two?</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// GUIDANCE: recommend two | REASONING: redundancy ensures you are not dependent on laundry schedule | AUTONOMY: washing one while wearing the other&gt;</p> <p>Avyanna: [to vendor] Two. Please.</p> <hr> <p>[The vendor cuts the fabric, wraps it, quotes a price. Avyanna&#x27;s hands shake as she pulls out the credit chit Vesper gave her.]</p> <p>(This is mine. I&#x27;m buying this with money that&#x27;s mine.)</p> <p>[The transaction completes. The vendor hands her a wrapped package. Fabric that she chose, in a color she wanted, for reasons that belong to her.]</p> <p>Vendor: [kindly] First purchase?</p> <p>Avyanna: [clutching the package] Yes.</p> <p>Vendor: [smiling] May it be the first of many.</p> <hr> <p>[They move through the market. Bubbles guides. Avyanna follows, holding her package like it might evaporate.]</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// OBSERVATION: trainee successfully completed transaction | ASSESSMENT: autonomy exercised | NEXT OBJECTIVE: non-essential purchase | PURPOSE: establish that wants are valid, not just needs&gt;</p> <p>Avyanna: [confused] Non-essential?</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// CLARIFICATION: something you want but do not strictly need | EXAMPLES: jewelry, decoration, comfort item | PURPOSE: learning that pleasure is allowed&gt;</p> <p>[Avyanna stops walking.]</p> <p>Avyanna: [quiet] I don&#x27;t know how to want things.</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// RESPONSE: then we practice | PROCEDURE: look at five items | REPORT: which one makes you curious&gt;</p> <hr> <p>[They browse. Avyanna looks without touching, without committing. A thousand small things she doesn&#x27;t understand the purpose of.]</p> <p>[And then she sees it.]</p> <p>[A small carved stone. Smooth. Dark. Polished to a shine. It sits on a vendor&#x27;s table among other small things, but this one catches light in a way that makes her stop.]</p> <p>[She stares at it.]</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// OBSERVATION: trainee fixated on object | QUERY: does this item interest you&gt;</p> <p>Avyanna: [hesitant] It&#x27;s... I don&#x27;t know what it is.</p> <p>Vendor: [nearby] Worry stone. You hold it when you&#x27;re stressed. Rub your thumb across it. Some people find it grounding.</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// ANALYSIS: tactile comfort object | FUNCTION: non-pharmaceutical anxiety reduction | ASSESSMENT: appropriate for trainee&gt;</p> <p>Avyanna: [touching it carefully] It&#x27;s smooth.</p> <p>Vendor: [watching her] It&#x27;s yours if you want it. Five credits.</p> <hr> <p>[Avyanna picks it up. The stone is warm from sitting in artificial sunlight. It fits in her palm. Her thumb finds a natural groove.]</p> <p>[She doesn&#x27;t need this. She knows she doesn&#x27;t need it.]</p> <p>(But I want it.)</p> <p>[The thought is terrifying. Wanting something for no practical reason. Wanting something just because it feels good to hold.]</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// GUIDANCE: this is called preference | IT IS ALLOWED | YOU MAY PURCHASE ITEMS THAT SERVE NO FUNCTION BEYOND COMFORT | THIS IS NOT WASTEFUL | THIS IS BEING HUMAN&gt;</p> <p>Avyanna: [to vendor, voice shaking] I&#x27;ll take it.</p> <hr> <p>[The transaction is quick. Five credits. The vendor wraps the stone in soft cloth, places it in a small pouch.]</p> <p>Vendor: [handing it over] May it bring you peace.</p> <p>[Avyanna takes the pouch. Opens it. Takes out the stone. Holds it.]</p> <p>[Her thumb finds the groove. Rubs. The motion is soothing in a way she didn&#x27;t know she needed.]</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// OBSERVATION: trainee purchased non-essential item for personal comfort | ASSESSMENT: autonomy developing | EMOTIONAL STATE: positive affect detected | STATUS: this is growth&gt;</p> <hr> <p>[They continue through the market. Avyanna buys a few more things: soap that smells like something other than industrial cleaner. A hair tie that isn&#x27;t a piece of wire. Socks that fit.]</p> <p>[Each purchase is easier than the last. Each choice is hers.]</p> <p>[And then the panic hits.]</p> <hr> <p>[Too many people. Too much noise. The walls are too far away and she can&#x27;t find the exits and her breathing is wrong and-]</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// ALERT: trainee entering panic state | RESPONSE: immediate extraction protocol | PROCEDURE: grounding sequence initiated&gt;</p> <p>[Bubbles moves between Avyanna and the crowd, projecting a soft light pattern on its surface. Steady. Rhythmic. Something to focus on.]</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// GROUNDING: focus on the light | BREATHE: in for four, hold for four, out for four | REMINDER: you are safe | REMINDER: i am here&gt;</p> <p>[Avyanna focuses on the light. Breathes. Four counts. Hold. Four counts out.]</p> <p>[The crowd is still there. The noise is still there. But Bubbles is there too, and the light is steady, and she can breathe.]</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// QUERY: do you want to leave&gt;</p> <p>Avyanna: [shaking] Yes. Please.</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// ACKNOWLEDGED: extracting to quiet zone | ROUTE: optimized for minimal crowd contact | DURATION: two minutes&gt;</p> <hr> <p>[Bubbles leads her out. The route is efficient, gentle, keeps her away from the densest crowds. They emerge into a quieter dock area, and Avyanna collapses onto a bench.]</p> <p>[She&#x27;s shaking. Her packages are clutched to her chest. The worry stone is in her hand, thumb rubbing the groove.]</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// ASSESSMENT: panic attack, moderate severity | STATUS: resolving | PROCEDURE: remain present, do not leave trainee alone&gt;</p> <p>[Bubbles hovers beside the bench. Silent. Present. Not demanding anything.]</p> <p>[Avyanna breathes. Counts. Rubs the stone. Slowly, the panic recedes.]</p> <p>Avyanna: [hoarse] Sorry.</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// RESPONSE: you have nothing to apologize for | OBSERVATION: sensory overload is common in post-extraction recovery | ASSESSMENT: you did well | YOU ASKED TO LEAVE | THAT IS PROGRESS&gt;</p> <hr> <p>[Avyanna looks at her packages. Fabric. Stone. Soap. Socks. Hair tie.]</p> <p>[Things she chose. Things that are hers.]</p> <p>Avyanna: [quiet] I bought things.</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// CONFIRMATION: yes | ASSESSMENT: you exercised autonomy in material choices | YOU DETERMINED YOUR OWN PREFERENCES | THIS IS OWNERSHIP&gt;</p> <p>Avyanna: [clutching the stone] In the mine, we didn&#x27;t... own anything. If you had something, someone could take it. So you didn&#x27;t have things.</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// ACKNOWLEDGMENT: scarcity as control mechanism | OBSERVATION: the mine taught you that ownership is temporary and painful | CORRECTION: here, ownership is protected | THESE ITEMS ARE YOURS | NO ONE WILL TAKE THEM&gt;</p> <hr> <p>[Avyanna sits with that. The stone in her hand. The packages in her lap.]</p> <p>(Mine. These are mine.)</p> <p>[The thought is huge. Terrifying. Liberating.]</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// OBSERVATION: you are learning what it means to have things | NEXT LESSON: learning what it means to have choices | ASSESSMENT: you are doing well | STATUS: i am proud of you&gt;</p> <p>[Avyanna reads the words. Her vision blurs.]</p> <p>Avyanna: [whispered] Thank you. For coming with me.</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// RESPONSE: that is what crew does | YOU ARE NOT ALONE | YOU WILL NEVER BE ALONE AGAIN&gt;</p> <hr> <p>[They sit in the quiet dock area for ten more minutes. Avyanna breathing. Bubbles hovering. The packages sitting safe in her lap.]</p> <p>[Eventually, Avyanna stands. Her legs are shaky but functional.]</p> <p>Avyanna: [to Bubbles] Ready to go back.</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// ACKNOWLEDGED: returning to lumen thief | ROUTE: direct, low-traffic | OBSERVATION: you did well today | YOU CHOSE THINGS FOR YOURSELF | THAT MATTERS&gt;</p> <p>[They walk back to the ship. Avyanna&#x27;s thumb never stops rubbing the worry stone.]</p> <p>[When they reach her quarters, she carefully unpacks everything. Lays it out on her bunk. Fabric. Stone. Soap. Socks. Hair tie.]</p> <p>[Hers.]</p> <hr> <p>&lt;CINNAMON.EXE// INVENTORY LOG: trainee acquired personal property | ITEMS: clothing material, comfort object, hygiene supplies, accessories | ASSESSMENT: appropriate selections | AUTONOMY: developing&gt;</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// PERSONAL NOTE: she panicked, but she asked for help | SHE BOUGHT SOMETHING JUST BECAUSE IT FELT GOOD | this is what healing looks like&gt;</p> <hr> <p>*End Scene*</p> </div> * [[Continue to optional scene 10b: Roster Roles And Refusal->Act1 10b Roster Roles And Refusal]] * [[Continue to Chapter 11: Shard Whispers->Act1 11 Shard Whispers]] * [[Return to route map->Act 1 Route Map]]<h1>Optional 10b: Roster Roles And Refusal</h1> <p class="source-note">Source: 10b_roster_roles_and_refusal.md</p> <div class="scene-prose"> <p>&gt; **[SCENE - OCCURS POST-CH10, FIRST WEEK ABOARD]** &gt; *Location: Atrium / Commons message board* &gt; *Present: Avyanna, Bubbles, Cinnamon.exe (Waffle cameo)* &gt; *Teaching: how the ship uses roles without cages; how to refuse without debt-math*</p> <hr> <p>[The atrium looks like a public square that got lost inside a ship.]</p> <p>[A wall of screens. A board covered in handwritten notes and printed forms. A rack of tools that are treated like they belong to everyone.]</p> <p>[Avyanna stands close enough to read but not close enough to be accused of wanting.]</p> <p>(If I stand too close, someone will notice and decide I need a job.)</p> <p>[Bubbles drifts in beside her, a soft whir of fans and servo-corrections.]</p> <p>Bubbles: You are reading the roster.</p> <p>Avyanna: [quick] I wasn&#x27;t- I just-</p> <p>[She stops herself.]</p> <p>[Elia taught her this: don&#x27;t spend breath paying for a harmless action.]</p> <p>Avyanna: I am reading it.</p> <p>[Bubbles pauses-an AI version of approval that doesn&#x27;t try to look like a smile.]</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// OBSERVATION: corrected speech pattern | INFERENCE: trainee adopting non-apology | CONFIDENCE: 0.83&gt;</p> <p>Bubbles: Good.</p> <p>[The roster is color-coded. Not names, not ranks. Tasks.]</p> <p>[*Bridge watch.*]</p> <p>[*Galley sanitation.*]</p> <p>[*Medbay inventory.*]</p> <p>[*Cargo checks.*]</p> <p>[*&quot;Sparkles&quot; review.*]</p> <p>[Next to each: a little note stamped in a hand that looks like it enjoys rules.]</p> <p>&gt; **ASSIGNED** / **VOLUNTEERED** / **PENDING**</p> <p>Avyanna: [frowning] Why does it say pending.</p> <p>[Cinnamon&#x27;s interface lights on the nearest terminal, text crisp as a knife.]</p> <p>&lt;CINNAMON.EXE// EXPLANATION: tasks require consent | NOTE: assignment without consent is &quot;order&quot; | ORDER is not default on this ship&gt;</p> <p>Avyanna: [quiet] But... things have to get done.</p> <p>&lt;CINNAMON.EXE// RESPONSE: yes | FOLLOW-UP: &quot;done&quot; is not the same as &quot;taken&quot;&gt;</p> <p>[Bubbles bumps the edge of the board with one rotor, almost gentle.]</p> <p>Bubbles: Sometimes the task waits.</p> <p>Avyanna: That seems... inefficient.</p> <p>Bubbles: Correct.</p> <p>Bubbles: We still do it.</p> <p>[Avyanna looks down at a section titled CREW ROLES.]</p> <p>[*Captain.*]</p> <p>[*Second.*]</p> <p>[*Pilot.*]</p> <p>[*Investigator.*]</p> <p>[*Medical.*]</p> <p>[*Heavy.*]</p> <p>[*Citizens.*]</p> <p>[She flinches at the word the mine used-ASSET-and finds it nowhere.]</p> <p>(No one owns me here. They keep saying it like I can just... accept it.)</p> <p>Avyanna: If there&#x27;s a captain, isn&#x27;t that... hierarchy.</p> <p>[Bubbles tilts, a micro-correction like shrugging.]</p> <p>Bubbles: There is leadership.</p> <p>Bubbles: There is not ownership.</p> <p>&lt;CINNAMON.EXE// CLARIFICATION: &quot;captain&quot; = accountability handle | NOT &quot;captain&quot; = entitlement to bodies&gt;</p> <p>Avyanna: [reading] &quot;If you do not understand a task, you may refuse.&quot;</p> <p>Avyanna: That&#x27;s... written down.</p> <p>&lt;CINNAMON.EXE// OBSERVATION: trainee surprise at explicit permissions | INFERENCE: prior environment punished questions | CONFIDENCE: 0.92&gt;</p> <p>[The board has a second line beneath it, in messier handwriting.]</p> <p>&gt; **If you refuse, you are still crew.**</p> <p>[Avyanna stares at that until her eyes sting.]</p> <p>Avyanna: What if refusing gets someone hurt.</p> <p>[Bubbles&#x27; fans shift. A sound like a small sigh.]</p> <p>Bubbles: Then we discuss it.</p> <p>Avyanna: In the mine, refusal was... a cost.</p> <p>&lt;CINNAMON.EXE// RESPONSE: on this ship, refusal is information&gt;</p> <p>Bubbles: And information prevents injury.</p> <p>[Avyanna&#x27;s throat tightens. She swallows it down.]</p> <p>Avyanna: So I can say no.</p> <p>Bubbles: Yes.</p> <p>Avyanna: And no one- [she can&#x27;t finish the sentence, because saying it out loud feels like inviting it]</p> <p>&lt;CINNAMON.EXE// COMPLETION: no penalty&gt;</p> <p>[The words sit there on the screen, plain and unornamented.]</p> <p>[No penalty.]</p> <p>[Avyanna reads it twice.]</p> <p>Avyanna: [small] That&#x27;s a lie in most places.</p> <p>&lt;CINNAMON.EXE// ACKNOWLEDGEMENT: correct&gt;</p> <p>&lt;WAFFLE.BAT// INTERRUPTION: it is a lie elsewhere | HERE it is a policy | POLICY is enforceable by civic annoyance&gt;</p> <p>[Waffle&#x27;s text appears with a little bat icon, like it&#x27;s proud of being a problem.]</p> <p>Avyanna: Civic annoyance.</p> <p>&lt;WAFFLE.BAT// DEFINITION: if someone punishes you for refusal, we punish them for punishing you&gt;</p> <p>[Bubbles rotates a fraction, as if to say: yes, that.]</p> <p>Bubbles: This is how communities function.</p> <p>[Avyanna points to a line item: GALLEY SANITATION.]</p> <p>Avyanna: Does someone get punished for not doing that.</p> <p>&lt;CINNAMON.EXE// RESPONSE: no | NOTE: consequences may include: smell | communal disappointment | Elia&#x27;s dramatic commentary&gt;</p> <p>Avyanna: Who enforces it.</p> <p>[Bubbles&#x27; fans change pitch: pleased.]</p> <p>Bubbles: You are learning.</p> <p>Avyanna: [dry, surprised at herself] I&#x27;m trying.</p> <p>&lt;CINNAMON.EXE// ADDENDUM: attempting is sufficient | PROGRESS TRACKING: consistent&gt;</p> <p>[Avyanna looks back at the roster.]</p> <p>[A new item appears at the bottom, stamped PENDING.]</p> <p>&gt; **TRAINEE: AVYANNA - OPTIONAL: LIBRARY ORIENTATION**</p> <p>[Avyanna stiffens on instinct.]</p> <p>Avyanna: Optional means... trap.</p> <p>&lt;CINNAMON.EXE// CORRECTION: optional means optional&gt;</p> <p>Bubbles: Do you want to go.</p> <p>[Avyanna hesitates.]</p> <p>(Do I want anything yet. Or do I just want to not be punished.)</p> <p>Avyanna: I want... to see it.</p> <p>Bubbles: Then we go.</p> <p>[No ceremony. No test. Just motion.]</p> <p>[Avyanna follows, and the ship does not demand payment for curiosity.]</p> <hr> <p>[They don&#x27;t go to the library.]</p> <p>[Not yet.]</p> <p>[Bubbles stops at a terminal alcove beside the board. Cinnamon&#x27;s interface is already open, a blank form waiting like a mouth.]</p> <p>Avyanna: What&#x27;s that.</p> <p>&lt;CINNAMON.EXE// ANSWER: practice&gt;</p> <p>[A new message appears on the screen, stamped with urgency that looks official on purpose.]</p> <p>&gt; **NOTICE** &gt; Report to medbay immediately. &gt; Failure to comply will be noted.</p> <p>[Avyanna&#x27;s body moves before her mind does.]</p> <p>[Her spine goes rigid. Her tongue prepares a yes.]</p> <p>[She catches herself with both hands on the counter.]</p> <p>Avyanna: [tight] Who sent that.</p> <p>&lt;CINNAMON.EXE// RESPONSE: me&gt;</p> <p>[Avyanna&#x27;s breath punches out.]</p> <p>Avyanna: You-</p> <p>&lt;CINNAMON.EXE// CLARIFICATION: simulation | PURPOSE: teach refusal without punishment&gt;</p> <p>[Bubbles drifts closer.]</p> <p>Bubbles: This is a drill.</p> <p>Avyanna: [quiet] Drills are still... real in the body.</p> <p>Bubbles: Correct.</p> <p>Bubbles: That is why we drill.</p> <p>&lt;CINNAMON.EXE// INSTRUCTION: apply three questions&gt;</p> <p>Avyanna: [swallowing] What. Why. What if.</p> <p>Avyanna: What: &quot;Report to medbay.&quot;</p> <p>Avyanna: Why: unknown.</p> <p>Avyanna: What if I don&#x27;t: &quot;noted.&quot; Not... specified.</p> <p>&lt;CINNAMON.EXE// AFFIRMATION: correct&gt;</p> <p>[Avyanna lifts her gaze to the terminal, to the lie that looks like authority.]</p> <p>Avyanna: Under which jurisdiction.</p> <p>&lt;CINNAMON.EXE// RESPONSE: internal ship message board&gt;</p> <p>Avyanna: Enforced by whom.</p> <p>&lt;CINNAMON.EXE// RESPONSE: no enforcement mechanism exists&gt;</p> <p>[Her mouth is dry.]</p> <p>Avyanna: So I can... ignore it.</p> <p>Bubbles: Yes.</p> <p>Avyanna: Or ask.</p> <p>Bubbles: Yes.</p> <p>[A second line appears beneath the notice, like a footnote that learned to bite.]</p> <p>&gt; *If this is urgent, reply with: WHAT IS THE EMERGENCY.*</p> <p>[Avyanna reads it twice.]</p> <p>Avyanna: That&#x27;s allowed.</p> <p>&lt;CINNAMON.EXE// ANSWER: yes&gt;</p> <p>Avyanna: [testing the muscle] What is the emergency.</p> <p>&lt;CINNAMON.EXE// RESPONSE: none&gt;</p> <p>[Her shoulders drop a fraction.]</p> <p>Avyanna: [quiet] That feels... impossible.</p> <p>Bubbles: It is merely unfamiliar.</p> <p>[Waffle&#x27;s icon pops up.]</p> <p>&lt;WAFFLE.BAT// COMMENTARY: you are doing it | CHAOS SCORE: 2/10 | DISAPPOINTMENT: mild&gt;</p> <p>Avyanna: [startled laugh] What.</p> <p>&lt;WAFFLE.BAT// EXPLANATION: chaos is educational | HOWEVER: this is healthy boring&gt;</p> <hr> <p>[Cinnamon opens another template.]</p> <p>&gt; **REFUSAL RECORD (INTERNAL)** &gt; *Request:* &gt; *Your response:* &gt; *Reason (optional):* &gt; *Follow-up action:*</p> <p>Avyanna: You keep records of... no.</p> <p>&lt;CINNAMON.EXE// AFFIRMATION: yes&gt;</p> <p>Avyanna: Why.</p> <p>&lt;CINNAMON.EXE// EXPLANATION: patterns | NOTE: if someone repeatedly &quot;requests&quot; in coercive shapes, we notice&gt;</p> <p>[Avyanna&#x27;s stomach twists.]</p> <p>Avyanna: So if someone starts acting like Aurum-</p> <p>Bubbles: We notice.</p> <p>Bubbles: Early.</p> <p>Avyanna: And then.</p> <p>&lt;WAFFLE.BAT// ANSWER: then we get annoying&gt;</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// ADDENDUM: then we protect you&gt;</p> <p>[Avyanna stares at that.]</p> <p>(Protect. Not own.)</p> <p>Avyanna: Do you... record when I say yes too.</p> <p>&lt;CINNAMON.EXE// RESPONSE: only when &quot;yes&quot; affects safety | NOTE: consent records are not ownership records&gt;</p> <p>Avyanna: Consent records.</p> <p>Bubbles: You are allowed to have a history that no one can edit without effort.</p> <p>[The sentence lands like a door that locks from the inside.]</p> <hr> <p>[Avyanna looks back up at the board.]</p> <p>[At the &quot;sparkles&quot; section-complaints and suggestions, filed like civic petitions.]</p> <p>Avyanna: Are those... real.</p> <p>Bubbles: Yes.</p> <p>Avyanna: People complain.</p> <p>Bubbles: Frequently.</p> <p>Avyanna: And no one-</p> <p>&lt;CINNAMON.EXE// COMPLETION: retaliates&gt;</p> <p>[Avyanna swallows.]</p> <p>Avyanna: That&#x27;s... a lot of trust.</p> <p>&lt;CINNAMON.EXE// RESPONSE: trust is built by enforcing it&gt;</p> <p>Avyanna: Who enforces it.</p> <p>[Bubbles tilts.]</p> <p>Bubbles: Us.</p> <p>&lt;WAFFLE.BAT// ADDENDUM: and also social disapproval&gt;</p> <p>Avyanna: [soft] Civic annoyance.</p> <p>&lt;WAFFLE.BAT// AFFIRMATION: civic annoyance&gt;</p> <p>[Avyanna looks at the empty space on the board where her own note could go, someday.]</p> <p>(A voice that doesn&#x27;t have to be purchased.)</p> <p>[She doesn&#x27;t post yet.]</p> <p>[But she stands there long enough that the ship records the possibility and doesn&#x27;t demand payment for it either.]</p> </div> * [[Continue to Chapter 11: Shard Whispers->Act1 11 Shard Whispers]] * [[Return to route map->Act 1 Route Map]]<h1>Chapter 11: Shard Whispers</h1> <p class="source-note">Source: 11_shard_whispers.md</p> <div class="scene-prose"> <p>&gt; **LUMEN THIEF - MEDICAL LOG (RESTRICTED):** &gt; *Patient: Avyanna Lagrange* &gt; *Attending: Nyx Halden, Waffle.bat (assist)* &gt; *Subject: Embedded foreign material, spinal column* &gt; *Status: Integrated. Non-removable. Active.* &gt; *Note: &quot;Active&quot; is underselling it.*</p> <hr> <p>[Two weeks aboard. Avyanna is learning to eat without permission. Learning to sleep without her boots on. Learning the shape of a life that doesn&#x27;t hurt.]</p> <p>[The shard is learning too.]</p> <hr> <p>[It starts with heat.]</p> <p>[Not fever-something sharper. Localized. A warmth that blooms at the base of her skull and radiates down her spine like spreading honey.]</p> <p>[Avyanna pauses mid-step in the corridor. Back to the bulkhead without thinking.]</p> <p>[Two exits. One blind corner. One camera dome she still can&#x27;t stop looking for, even when there&#x27;s nothing there.]</p> <p>[She presses two fingers to the base of her skull, as if she can pin the sensation down.]</p> <p>[Waits. The heat fades.]</p> <p>(Nothing. Just... adjustment. The body adapting.)</p> <p>[She keeps walking. Doesn&#x27;t mention it to anyone.]</p> <hr> <p>[Medical bay. Nyx&#x27;s domain-cluttered with equipment, crystals, and datapads covered in handwriting that looks like equations had a fight with poetry.]</p> <p>[Avyanna sits on the examination table. The scanner hums around her-not the cold, indifferent hum of the Kennel&#x27;s medical station, but something warmer. More attentive.]</p> <p>[She keeps her hands flat on her thighs. Palms down. Like that will keep her from shaking.]</p> <p>[Her eyes keep drifting to the door. Not fear exactly. Logistics. If it opens, she wants to be facing it.]</p> <p>&lt;WAFFLE.BAT// SCAN-STATUS: deep tissue | SUBJECT: spinal integration | OBSERVATION: heart rate elevated | INFERENCE: nervous | CONFIDENCE: 0.77 | NOTE: expected&gt;</p> <p>Nyx: [still watching the readouts] If you want me to stop the scan, say so. No penalty. No explaining required.</p> <p>Avyanna: [after a beat] Don&#x27;t stop.</p> <p>Nyx: [studying readouts, brow furrowed] The markings on your spine-we knew they were connected to the shard. What we didn&#x27;t know was how deep the connection goes.</p> <p>Avyanna: [carefully] How deep does it go?</p> <p>Nyx: [a pause] Cellular.</p> <p>[The word hangs in the air.]</p> <p>Avyanna: What does that mean?</p> <p>Nyx: [turning to face her] The shard isn&#x27;t just embedded in you. It&#x27;s integrated. Bonded at a level we can&#x27;t surgically address. It&#x27;s become part of your nervous system.</p> <p>[Avyanna&#x27;s hands grip the edge of the table. The Kennel taught her what &quot;permanent&quot; meant. It meant trapped. It meant no escape.]</p> <p>Avyanna: [voice thin] Can you remove it?</p> <p>Nyx: [honest, gentle] No. Not without killing you. And honestly, at this point, I&#x27;m not sure where you end and it begins.</p> <p>[Silence. The scanner continues its quiet work.]</p> <p>Avyanna: [barely audible] Is it going to kill me?</p> <p>Nyx: [considering, then] I don&#x27;t think so. The integration is stable. Your vitals are actually improving-better than when we first scanned you. It&#x27;s not attacking you.</p> <p>Avyanna: Then what is it doing?</p> <p>Nyx: [a small smile, something like wonder] I think it chose you.</p> <p>[The words don&#x27;t make sense. Things don&#x27;t choose in the Kennel. Things are assigned, allocated, extracted.]</p> <p>Avyanna: Things don&#x27;t choose.</p> <p>Nyx: [mild] Shards do. This one, at least. It could have bonded with anyone who touched it-but it didn&#x27;t activate until you. It waited. And now it&#x27;s... settling in.</p> <p>[Avyanna looks at her hands. Normal hands. Human hands. Nothing visible to suggest there&#x27;s something ancient living inside her.]</p> <p>Nyx: [softer] This is unprecedented. I&#x27;ve studied Lattice phenomena for decades, and I&#x27;ve never seen anything like this. Whatever you&#x27;re carrying-it&#x27;s old. Pre-Synthetic, maybe-older than the protocols most people use without thinking. And it seems to want to be here.</p> <hr> <p>[That night, she dreams.]</p> <p>[Not the nightmares of the Kennel-the extraction warnings, the shift-change sirens, the faces of workers who didn&#x27;t wake up. Something else.]</p> <p>[Geometric patterns. Crystalline structures that fold in on themselves, impossibly complex. A hum that might be a voice if she could just hear it properly.]</p> <p>[Not words. A tone shift. Like the hum changes shape when she thinks certain thoughts.]</p> <p>[Heat that isn&#x27;t comfort so much as presence. Attention without hands.]</p> <p>[She wakes gasping, but not afraid. The presence behind her eyes pulses once-a brief rise in temperature, then neutral-and settles back into silence.]</p> <p>(You&#x27;re there. You&#x27;ve always been there. Since the rock.)</p> <p>[No answer. Just patience. Just waiting.]</p> <hr> <p>[Over the next days, it stops feeling random.]</p> <p>[Heat, yes-but not always. Sometimes the sensation is a brief cooling, like something withdrawing its attention. Sometimes it&#x27;s pressure behind her eyes, gentle until it isn&#x27;t, like a hand held up: stop.]</p> <p>[The patterns start to organize. Not readable, but repeatable. Shapes that return in the same order, the way a hallway returns in your dreams.]</p> <p>[And the hum has moods. Flat when she&#x27;s exhausted. Sharper when she&#x27;s afraid. Almost curious when she lets herself notice the ship&#x27;s kindness without immediately looking for the hook.]</p> <p>(Temperature. Pressure. Pattern. Tone.)</p> <p>(If she were logging it, that&#x27;s what she&#x27;d write.)</p> <p>[She tests it without meaning to.]</p> <p>[When she lies-small, easy lies, the ones that kept her alive-heat blooms and then cools fast, like a correction.]</p> <p>[Maybe it&#x27;s coincidence. Maybe she just wants it to be a responder instead of a symptom.]</p> <p>[When she thinks, very carefully, about running-about finding an exit she can control-the pressure spikes behind her eyes until she sits down and breathes and lets the thought go.]</p> <p>[She can&#x27;t prove correlation is anything but correlation. But she knows the sensations change when her choices change.]</p> <hr> <p>[She hides it.]</p> <p>[Of course she hides it. The Kennel taught her that weakness is leverage. That anything unusual about you becomes a resource for someone else to exploit.]</p> <p>(If I&#x27;m broken, they might send me back.)</p> <p>[She knows it&#x27;s not rational. They&#x27;ve adopted her. Given her a name. But the Kennel&#x27;s logic runs deep, and trust is still a foreign language.]</p> <p>[So when Rho asks how she&#x27;s feeling, she says &quot;fine.&quot;]</p> <p>[When Vesper notices she&#x27;s distracted, she says &quot;just tired.&quot;]</p> <p>[When Elia&#x27;s gaze lingers a moment too long, she looks away and pretends there&#x27;s nothing to see.]</p> <p>[She also starts choosing seats with her back to a wall. Starts keeping one hand wrapped around a mug at all times, like it can anchor her. Starts wiping her palms on her pants before anyone can notice they&#x27;re damp.]</p> <hr> <p>[Inventory. Cargo bay. The familiar work of counting and cataloging-something her hands can do while her mind wrestles with the thing in her spine.]</p> <p>[A heat flare. Stronger than usual. She sways, catches herself on a crate.]</p> <p>(Not now. Please not now.)</p> <p>[The geometric patterns bloom across her vision-fractals, lattices, structures that feel like they&#x27;re trying to tell her something. The pressure builds behind her eyes.]</p> <p>[The overhead lights stutter once. Less than a heartbeat, but enough to make her stomach drop.]</p> <p>[Her tablet chirps with a system alert. She slaps it silent without looking.]</p> <p>&lt;WAFFLE.BAT// OBSERVATION: sensor jitter 0.7s | SOURCE: cargo bay | INFERENCE: resonance interference | CONFIDENCE: 0.62 | COMMENT: new. monitoring.&gt;</p> <p>[She closes them. Breathes. Waits for it to pass.]</p> <p>[When she opens them, Bubbles is there.]</p> <p>[The drone hovers at the edge of the aisle-not close enough to intrude, close enough to respond. Scarred metal, purposeful presence. She doesn&#x27;t remember hearing it approach.]</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// OBSERVATION: distress markers | DURATION: 47 seconds | INTERVENTION: proximity only&gt;</p> <p>Bubbles: [quiet, through the speaker] You don&#x27;t have to tell anyone right now.</p> <p>[Avyanna freezes. Caught.]</p> <p>Bubbles: But I&#x27;m here.</p> <p>[Not a demand. Not a report. Just presence. Just witness.]</p> <p>Avyanna: [after a long moment] You saw.</p> <p>Bubbles: I see a lot of things. [beat] I don&#x27;t always report them.</p> <p>Avyanna: [confused] Why?</p> <p>Bubbles: [the drone shifts slightly, something almost like a shrug] Because sometimes people need time to figure out what they&#x27;re experiencing before they have to explain it.</p> <p>[Avyanna stares at the scarred drone. In the Kennel, being observed meant being documented. Being documented meant being controlled.]</p> <p>Bubbles: [gentler] The thing in you-it&#x27;s not hurting you, is it?</p> <p>Avyanna: [automatic] I&#x27;m fine.</p> <p>[The word comes out before she can stop it.]</p> <p>Avyanna: [lower] It doesn&#x27;t hurt. I don&#x27;t think. It just... makes me dizzy sometimes.</p> <p>Bubbles: And you&#x27;re not ready to talk about it.</p> <p>[A statement, not a question. Avyanna nods.]</p> <p>Bubbles: Then we wait. [beat] When you&#x27;re ready, I&#x27;ll be here. So will Nyx. So will everyone else.</p> <p>[The drone doesn&#x27;t move away. Just hovers, patient, present.]</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// STATUS: guardian protocol active | SUBJECT: Avyanna | INFERENCE: trust developing (incremental) | CONFIDENCE: 0.63 | NOTE: we&#x27;ll get there.&gt;</p> <hr> <p>[Avyanna returns to her inventory. Hands moving, mind elsewhere.]</p> <p>(You trust the AI more than the humans. Why?)</p> <p>[She knows why. The AI doesn&#x27;t want anything from her. Bubbles isn&#x27;t calculating her value, measuring her utility, deciding whether she&#x27;s worth keeping. Bubbles is just... there. Present without demands.]</p> <p>[The humans are kind. Genuinely kind-she&#x27;s starting to believe that. But kindness from humans has always come with invisible strings. The Kennel taught her to look for them.]</p> <p>[The AIs don&#x27;t have strings. They have protocols, and the protocols are transparent.]</p> <p>(Is that the lesson? Trust the systems you can see?)</p> <p>[The presence behind her eyes stirs. Something that might be agreement. Something that might be encouragement.]</p> <p>(You&#x27;re watching me learn. You&#x27;re waiting for something.)</p> <p>[A small rise in temperature at the base of her skull. Not an answer-but not nothing, either.]</p> <hr> <p>[Late night. Her quarters. The viewport filled with stars.]</p> <p>[Avyanna lies in bed-still in her clothes, but boots off now, a compromise with safety-and stares at the ceiling.]</p> <p>[The geometric patterns flicker at the edge of her vision. Familiar now. Almost comforting.]</p> <p>(What are you trying to tell me?)</p> <p>[No words. But something shifts in the patterns-a sense of reaching, of trying to communicate across a gap that hasn&#x27;t quite closed yet.]</p> <p>(You can hear me. Can&#x27;t you?)</p> <p>[Heat. Then steadier pressure, like a hand bracing a door. Patience without language.]</p> <p>(But you can&#x27;t talk back. Not yet.)</p> <p>[The hum shifts tone. Not louder, just... edged. The patterns intensify briefly, then fade.]</p> <p>[The bedside light flickers once, then steadies.]</p> <p>&lt;WAFFLE.BAT// OBSERVATION: sensor latency spike 0.6s | SOURCE: Avyanna quarters | INFERENCE: interference signature match (cargo bay) | CONFIDENCE: 0.68&gt;</p> <p>(You&#x27;re trapped too. In a different way. Trying to get out. Trying to connect.)</p> <p>[The presence settles. Content with being understood, even partially.]</p> <p>[Avyanna closes her eyes. The patterns persist behind her eyelids-beautiful, incomprehensible, waiting.]</p> <p>(I don&#x27;t know what you are. I don&#x27;t know what you want.)</p> <p>(But you chose me. That has to mean something.)</p> <p>[Sleep comes eventually. The presence watches, patient as stone, ancient as stars.]</p> <p>[Somewhere in the ship&#x27;s systems, Bubbles logs a quiet note:]</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// NIGHT-WATCH: Avyanna | SLEEP-STATUS: achieved | ANOMALIES: minor (sensor jitter 0.7s) | INFERENCE: acclimation (incremental) | CONFIDENCE: 0.66 | NOTE: good. that&#x27;s how this works.&gt;</p> <hr> <p>*End Chapter 11*</p> </div> Where does the route go next? * [[Read optional scene 11a: Id Names Forms->Act1 11a Id Names Forms]] * [[Read optional scene 11b: Senna Small Good Thing->Act1 11b Senna Small Good Thing]] * [[Continue to Chapter 12: The Doctrine->Act1 12 The Doctrine]] * [[Return to route map->Act 1 Route Map]]<h1>Optional 11a: Id Names Forms</h1> <p class="source-note">Source: 11a_id_names_forms.md</p> <div class="scene-prose"> <p>&gt; **[SCENE - OCCURS BETWEEN CH11-12]** &gt; *Location: Lumen Thief secure conference room* &gt; *Present: Avyanna, Vesper, Elisira* &gt; *Teaching: Identity systems, paperwork as weapon, harm reduction through documentation*</p> <hr> <p>[The conference room is smaller than Avyanna expected. One table, three chairs, a secure terminal, and walls that Cinnamon assures her are soundproofed.]</p> <p>[Vesper sits with three different datapads arranged in front of her like cards in a game. Elisira stands near the door-not blocking it, just present.]</p> <p>[Avyanna sits across from Vesper and tries not to look like she&#x27;s calculating exits.]</p> <p>Vesper: [calm] We&#x27;re going to register you properly. Three systems. Three different identities. All legitimate. All protective.</p> <p>Avyanna: [confused] Three?</p> <p>Elisira: [from the door] Some people need five names to survive. Three is conservative.</p> <hr> <p>[Vesper taps the first datapad.]</p> <p>Vesper: System one: Rim Accord. This is your primary legal identity. The one that says you&#x27;re a free citizen with full autonomy. No indenture contracts. No ownership claims. No extraction permissions.</p> <p>[She pulls up a form. It&#x27;s written in plain language-none of the labyrinthine jargon Avyanna remembers from the mine&#x27;s paperwork.]</p> <p>Vesper: Name, birthdate, origin station, current residence, occupation. Standard fields. But here&#x27;s what matters: every piece of information you put here is public record. Anyone can request it. Compact, Guild, random busybody with a credit chit.</p> <p>Avyanna: [tense] So they can find me.</p> <p>Vesper: [precise] They can find this version of you. Which is why we&#x27;re strategic about what this version says.</p> <hr> <p>[Vesper walks through each field.]</p> <p>Vesper: Name. You&#x27;re using Avyanna Lagrange. That&#x27;s your choice. If you wanted to change it, now&#x27;s the time.</p> <p>Avyanna: [quiet] I want to keep it. It&#x27;s... mine.</p> <p>Vesper: [nodding] Understood. Birthdate. The mine didn&#x27;t track yours. So you pick one. Any date. It&#x27;s yours now.</p> <p>[Avyanna stares at the calendar field.]</p> <p>Avyanna: I don&#x27;t... how do I choose?</p> <p>Elisira: [dryly] Pick a date that makes you hard to profile. Not a holiday. Not a memorable anniversary. Something ordinary.</p> <p>Avyanna: [after a moment] Third month, fourteenth day. Standard year... I don&#x27;t know. Seventeen years ago?</p> <p>Vesper: [entering it] Close enough. We&#x27;ll backdate your age to seventeen. Young enough that gaps in your record are explainable, old enough that you have legal autonomy.</p> <hr> <p>Vesper: Origin station. This one&#x27;s strategic. We&#x27;re not listing Aurum Extraction. We&#x27;re listing Taelios Forge-a mid-tier manufacturing station with lousy record-keeping. If anyone checks, the station will shrug and say &quot;records incomplete due to fire in archive seven years ago.&quot;</p> <p>Avyanna: [slowly] You&#x27;re... lying?</p> <p>Vesper: [meeting her eyes] I&#x27;m protecting you with plausible deniability. Taelios is real. The fire is real. Your presence there is unfalsifiable because the records genuinely don&#x27;t exist.</p> <p>Elisira: [adding] This is harm reduction. We&#x27;re giving you a past that doesn&#x27;t make you a target.</p> <hr> <p>Vesper: Current residence: Lumen Thief, Guild registry 7742-KAEL. Occupation: apprentice evidence contractor.</p> <p>[She pauses.]</p> <p>Vesper: That last one is important. &quot;Apprentice&quot; means you&#x27;re learning, not experienced. Keeps expectations low. &quot;Evidence contractor&quot; means you work with the Guild but aren&#x27;t Guild property. You&#x27;re crew, not asset.</p> <p>Avyanna: [testing the words] Crew. Not asset.</p> <p>Vesper: [firm] Crew. With full autonomy. This form makes that official.</p> <hr> <p>[Vesper taps through the rest of the fields. Medical information: minimal disclosure. Education: &quot;station standard, records incomplete.&quot; Emergency contacts: Lumen Thief crew roster.]</p> <p>[She submits the form. A confirmation code appears.]</p> <p>Vesper: That&#x27;s identity one. Rim Accord now recognizes you as a free citizen. If anyone questions your status, you show them this registry number. It&#x27;s your proof of autonomy.</p> <p>[Avyanna stares at the number. Seventeen characters. Hers.]</p> <hr> <p>[Vesper switches to the second datapad.]</p> <p>Vesper: System two: Guild transit credentials. This one&#x27;s more restricted. Only Guild-affiliated stations can access it. But it gives you freedom of movement on Guild routes without customs hassles.</p> <p>[Another form. This one asks for biometrics.]</p> <p>Vesper: We&#x27;ll need a retinal scan, fingerprints, and a voiceprint. All stored encrypted. Only released to Guild checkpoints, and only when you authorize it.</p> <p>Avyanna: [wary] What if I don&#x27;t authorize it?</p> <p>Vesper: Then you don&#x27;t get through the checkpoint. But that&#x27;s your choice. You control when your biometrics are shared.</p> <hr> <p>[Elisira steps forward with a small scanner.]</p> <p>Elisira: This won&#x27;t hurt. Look here. Don&#x27;t blink.</p> <p>[Flash. Avyanna&#x27;s retina captured in a quarter-second. Fingerprints next-each finger pressed to a cool glass surface. Then voiceprint: Avyanna speaks her name, her registry number, and a randomized passphrase.]</p> <p>[All of it stored. All of it hers. All of it controlled by her consent.]</p> <p>Vesper: [submitting] Guild transit credentials issued. You can now move through Guild space with minimal friction.</p> <hr> <p>[Vesper picks up the third datapad. Pauses.]</p> <p>Vesper: System three is different. This one is... insurance.</p> <p>Avyanna: [sensing weight] Insurance?</p> <p>Vesper: [carefully] Some people need a name that isn&#x27;t connected to their primary identity. A name they can use when the first two are compromised. A name that lets them disappear if necessary.</p> <p>Elisira: [from the door] We call it an egress identity. A way out when the walls close in.</p> <hr> <p>[Avyanna&#x27;s stomach tightens.]</p> <p>Avyanna: You think I&#x27;ll need to disappear?</p> <p>Vesper: [honest] I think you might. The Compact knows you exist. Aurum Extraction knows you exist. Right now, they don&#x27;t know where you are. But if they find out, you&#x27;ll need options.</p> <p>[She slides the third datapad across the table.]</p> <p>Vesper: This identity is minimal. Different name. Different birthdate. Different origin. Same species, same approximate age, but everything else is fiction. We&#x27;ll register it with a neutral station that doesn&#x27;t share data automatically.</p> <p>Vesper: You&#x27;ll never use it unless you have to. But if you need it, it&#x27;ll be there.</p> <hr> <p>[Avyanna stares at the blank form.]</p> <p>Avyanna: What name do I use?</p> <p>Vesper: Your choice. Pick something that doesn&#x27;t emotionally attach to you. This identity is a tool, not a person.</p> <p>[Avyanna thinks. Names from the mine. Names from stations she&#x27;s seen. Something ordinary. Something forgettable.]</p> <p>Avyanna: [slowly] Kess Tam.</p> <p>Vesper: [approving] Good. Generic. Unremarkable. Birthdate?</p> <p>Avyanna: Eighth month, second day. Eighteen years ago.</p> <p>Vesper: Origin?</p> <p>Avyanna: [looking at Vesper] You choose. I don&#x27;t know what&#x27;s safe.</p> <p>Vesper: [noting] Miren&#x27;s Cradle. Agricultural station. Large population, high turnover, minimal surveillance. Perfect for ghosts.</p> <hr> <p>[They fill out the rest. Minimal details. No medical history. No education records. No emergency contacts. Just a name, a face, and a birthdate that might exist somewhere in the statistical noise of the galaxy.]</p> <p>[Vesper submits it. Another confirmation code. Another identity, stored and waiting.]</p> <p>Vesper: [meeting Avyanna&#x27;s eyes] You now have three names. Three versions of yourself, each with different utility and different exposure.</p> <p>Vesper: Avyanna Lagrange, Rim Accord citizen: this is who you are publicly.</p> <p>Vesper: Avyanna Lagrange, Guild contractor: this is who you are professionally.</p> <p>Vesper: Kess Tam: this is who you become if everything else fails.</p> <hr> <p>[Avyanna&#x27;s hands are shaking. Three names. Three lives. Three ways to exist.]</p> <p>Avyanna: [whispered] How do I remember which one to use?</p> <p>Elisira: [stepping forward] Practice. We&#x27;ll drill scenarios. &quot;You&#x27;re at a Rim station, customs asks for ID&quot;-you show Rim Accord. &quot;You&#x27;re at a Guild checkpoint&quot;-you show Guild credentials. &quot;You&#x27;re running and someone&#x27;s hunting you&quot;-you become Kess Tam and you don&#x27;t look back.</p> <hr> <p>[Vesper closes the datapads.]</p> <p>Vesper: Now the hard part.
