punished.)</p> <p>[She takes it anyway.]</p> <hr> <p>[They leave the office together.]</p> <p>[In the atrium, there is a public board with a section labeled RECEIPTS.]</p> <p>[Avyanna has seen other entries there: small disputes, supply issues, petty injustices documented in neat lines.]</p> <p>[Vesper pins one sanitized summary-no names that would get someone killed, just the shape of the crime and the evidence that it happened.]</p> <p>Vesper: [low] We don&#x27;t do this to be liked.</p> <p>Avyanna: [quiet] We do it because it&#x27;s true.</p> <p>Vesper: Yes.</p> <p>[A few crew members pause as they pass.]</p> <p>[They read.]</p> <p>[They don&#x27;t clap. They don&#x27;t cheer.]</p> <p>[They nod-like a vow acknowledged without noise.]</p> <p>[Avyanna stands beside Vesper and watches her own life become a public weight the mine can&#x27;t lift easily.]</p> <p>(This is what it feels like to have proof.)</p> <p>[The ship hums around them, holding the moment without trying to own it.]</p> <hr> <p>[Later, Avyanna takes her copy folder back to her quarters.]</p> <p>[She sets it on the small desk like it might bite.]</p> <p>[Then she opens it anyway.]</p> <p>[The first page is the sanitized summary Vesper pinned. The second is the internal index. The third is her own witness statement-her words, typed cleanly, no mine stutter, no apologies baked in.]</p> <p>[Avyanna reads it three times.]</p> <p>(If this existed when I was eight, would it have saved anyone.)</p> <p>[Her throat tightens.]</p> <p>[She reaches for her notebook and writes another claim.]</p> <p>&gt; **CLAIM:** I am allowed to survive.</p> <p>[She stares at it.]</p> <p>(Allowed by whom.)</p> <p>[She writes the next line carefully.]</p> <p>&gt; **ENFORCED BY:** us.</p> <p>[It looks childish.]</p> <p>[It looks like the truth anyway.]</p> <hr> <p>[A notification pings on her terminal.]</p> <p>&lt;CINNAMON.EXE// MESSAGE: archive replication complete | COPIES: 7 | LOCATION: distributed | NOTE: erasure now expensive&gt;</p> <p>Avyanna: [muttering] Good.</p> <p>[She sits back.]</p> <p>[The quiet in her room is different now.]</p> <p>[Not empty. Not waiting.]</p> <p>[Held.]</p> <p>[She can hear distant voices in the atrium-Elia laughing too loud, Rho complaining about the coffee machine like it&#x27;s a moral failing, Waffle&#x27;s text chattering across a screen somewhere.]</p> <p>[For a moment, the mine tries to overlay that sound with its own: sirens, boots, supervisors shouting numbers.]</p> <p>[Avyanna&#x27;s body tenses.]</p> <p>[Then she remembers the stamp.]</p> <p>*THUD.*</p> <p>[Receipt acknowledged.]</p> <p>[She breathes until the mine recedes.]</p> <hr> <p>[She slips the copy folder into the bottom drawer of her desk.]</p> <p>[Not hiding it.]</p> <p>[Storing it like something that will still be there when she needs it.]</p> <p>[She looks at the door.]</p> <p>[Still open, as ship policy.]</p> <p>[She doesn&#x27;t close it.]</p> <p>[But for the first time, she doesn&#x27;t stare at it like it&#x27;s a countdown either.]</p> <p>(I don&#x27;t have to be ready to run every second.)</p> <p>[She lies down.]</p> <p>[The pillow smells like clean fabric instead of dust.]</p> <p>[Her hands still tremble, but less.]</p> <p>[She falls asleep with the ugly, miraculous weight of proof in the drawer and the knowledge that the ship will keep humming whether she&#x27;s awake to guard it or not.]</p> <hr> <p>[When she wakes, the first thing her body does is listen.]</p> <p>[Not for sirens.]</p> <p>[For the ship.]</p> <p>[A steady hum. Footsteps in the corridor. A door hiss somewhere distant.]</p> <p>[No shouting. No quota bell. No supervisor voice turning time into threat.]</p> <p>[Her shoulders unclench one notch at a time.]</p> <p>(I slept.)</p> <p>[She sits up and waits for the panic that usually follows sleep.]</p> <p>[It comes, but smaller. Late. Like the mine is running out of power.]</p> <p>[Avyanna touches the back of her neck where her old tag used to itch.]</p> <p>[Then she stands and opens the drawer.]</p> <p>[The folder is still there.]</p> <p>[The paper didn&#x27;t vanish overnight. No one &quot;processed&quot; it away while she wasn&#x27;t looking.]</p> <p>[She closes the drawer again, not because she&#x27;s hiding it-because she&#x27;s practicing treating permanence as normal.]</p> <hr> <p>[In the galley, Rho is already at war with the coffee machine.]</p> <p>Rho: [to the machine] You are a liar and a coward.</p> <p>Elia: [from the counter] It&#x27;s just a machine.</p> <p>Rho: It knows what it did.</p> <p>[Avyanna pauses in the doorway, watching the scene like it&#x27;s a documentary about a species she&#x27;s joining.]</p> <p>(They argue and nobody dies.)</p> <p>[Bubbles notices her first, as usual.]</p> <p>Bubbles: Good morning.</p> <p>Avyanna: [quiet] Morning.</p> <p>[Rho turns, squints at her the way he squints at broken parts.]</p> <p>Rho: You sleep.</p> <p>Avyanna: [blinking] Yes.</p> <p>Rho: Good.</p> <p>[He pushes a plate toward her without asking.]</p> <p>[Eggs. Toast. Something green that might be an herb and not a punishment.]</p> <p>Avyanna: [tight] I didn&#x27;t-</p> <p>Rho: Eat.</p> <p>Avyanna: [catching herself] Why.</p> <p>Rho: Because you&#x27;re alive and it&#x27;s morning and you don&#x27;t have to earn breakfast.</p> <p>[Avyanna&#x27;s throat tightens.]</p> <p>Avyanna: Okay.</p> <p>[She sits.]</p> <p>[She eats one bite.]</p> <p>[Her body waits for the ledger to appear and demand repayment.]</p> <p>[Nothing happens.]</p> <p>[Elia watches her chew like it&#x27;s a magic trick.]</p> <p>Elia: See. The ship didn&#x27;t explode because you accepted food.</p> <p>Avyanna: [dry] Not yet.</p> <p>Elia: That&#x27;s the spirit.</p> <hr> <p>[Cinnamon&#x27;s text scrolls on the terminal above the galley counter.]</p> <p>&lt;CINNAMON.EXE// STATUS: evidence archive stable | NOTE: hostile systems will attempt narrative repair | SUGGESTION: remain observant&gt;</p> <p>Avyanna: Narrative repair.</p> <p>Elia: [cheerful] That means they&#x27;re going to lie louder.</p> <p>Rho: And send invoices for the privilege.</p> <p>[Avyanna swallows.]</p> <p>Avyanna: [quiet] And we keep receipts.</p> <p>[Vesper isn&#x27;t here.]</p> <p>[But the phrase feels like a rail anyway.]</p> <p>Bubbles: Yes.</p> <p>&lt;BUBBLES// OBSERVATION: trainee repeating doctrine without prompting | INFERENCE: internalization | CONFIDENCE: 0.83&gt;</p> <p>[Avyanna finishes her toast.]</p> <p>[She doesn&#x27;t thank anyone five times.]</p> <p>[She doesn&#x27;t apologize for taking up a chair.]</p> <p>[She just exists in the galley with the others, and the ship allows it like it&#x27;s always been the rule.]</p> <p>[Avyanna takes her mug to the sink when she&#x27;s done, because the ship culture prefers it, not because she&#x27;s afraid of being punished.]</p> <p>[She pauses with her hands in warm water, watching soap bubbles rise and pop.]</p> <p>(Small things can be safe too.)</p> <p>[She dries her hands and, without looking at anyone for permission, writes one line in her notebook:]</p> <p>&gt; **OBSERVATION:** breakfast arrived without an invoice.</p> <p>[Then she puts the notebook away and goes back to the table.]</p> <p>[No one tells her she&#x27;s taking too long.]</p> <p>[No one asks what she&#x27;s paying.]</p> <p>[The ship hums like a city that expects its citizens to eat, to wash dishes, to exist-and refuses to make any of it a moral trial.]</p> <p>[For now.]</p> </div> * [[Continue to optional scene 20d: Aleena Pulls Elia From Wreckage->Act1 20d Aleena Pulls Elia From Wreckage]] * [[Finish Act 1->Act 1 Ending]] * [[Return to route map->Act 1 Route Map]]<h1>Optional 20d: Aleena Pulls Elia From Wreckage</h1> <p class="source-note">Source: 20d_aleena_pulls_elia_from_wreckage.md</p> <div class="scene-prose"> <p>&gt; **[INTERSTITIAL - OCCURS POST-CH20]** &gt; *Trigger: Avyanna watches Elia move through the ship like it belongs to her* &gt; *Purpose: Aleena as origin of &quot;you coming?&quot; as invitation, not leash*</p> <hr> <p>[After &quot;The Receipt,&quot; the ship feels too bright.]</p> <p>[Not because the lights changed.]</p> <p>[Because Avyanna&#x27;s brain is doing what it always does when something impossible happens:]</p> <p>[Replaying it until it becomes survivable.]</p> <p>[She walks the corridor to bleed off the energy.]</p> <p>[She finds Elia in the maintenance bay, stripped down to a tank and cargo pants, wiping her blades with a cloth that looks too ordinary to be allowed near relic steel.]</p> <p>[Elia glances up and clocks Avyanna&#x27;s posture in half a second.]</p> <p>Elia: You&#x27;re vibrating.</p> <p>Avyanna: I&#x27;m not.</p> <p>Elia: You are.</p> <p>[Elia&#x27;s mouth quirks.]</p> <p>Elia: Want to break something.</p> <p>Avyanna: No.</p> <p>Elia: [delighted] Lies.</p> <p>[Avyanna surprises herself by almost smiling.]</p> <hr> <p>[Elia sets the cloth down.]</p> <p>Elia: What&#x27;s the spiral.</p> <p>Avyanna: Vesper said-</p> <p>[Avyanna stops.]</p> <p>[She doesn&#x27;t know how to say &quot;family&quot; without choking.]</p> <p>Elia: [softer, still blunt] Yeah.</p> <p>Elia: That&#x27;ll do it.</p> <p>[Avyanna&#x27;s gaze flicks to Elia&#x27;s hands.]</p> <p>[Scarred. Precise. Calm with weapons.]</p> <p>Avyanna: How did you get like this.</p> <p>[Elia blinks.]</p> <p>Elia: Like what.</p> <p>Avyanna: Like you&#x27;re... not scared.</p> <p>[Elia laughs once, sharp.]</p> <p>Elia: I&#x27;m terrified.</p> <p>Elia: I just don&#x27;t let it drive.</p> <p>[A beat.]</p> <p>Elia: Aleena taught me that.</p> <p>[The name lands with weight. Avyanna has heard it in scraps, in the way Elisira and Elia say it like a rule.]</p> <p>Avyanna: Who was she.</p> <p>[Elia&#x27;s eyes go distant.]</p> <hr> <p>## Intercut - Wreckage (Aleena Finds a Child)</p> <p>[Smoke.]</p> <p>[Hot metal.]</p> <p>[The kind of silence that comes after alarms die and before the survivors start screaming.]</p> <p>[Elia is a small bundle in the wreckage.]</p> <p>[Too young to understand what happened, only old enough to understand that everything hurts and no one is answering.]</p> <p>[A shadow falls over her.]</p> <p>[Not a supervisor shadow.]</p> <p>[A different kind: broad-shouldered, steady, moving through debris like it&#x27;s familiar.]</p> <p>[Aleena Lagrange kneels.]</p> <p>[Her face is smeared with soot. There is blood on her sleeve that isn&#x27;t hers. Her eyes are bright with anger that doesn&#x27;t spill onto the child.]</p> <p>[Aleena doesn&#x27;t reach in and grab.]</p> <p>[She clears a piece of twisted plating first.]</p> <p>[Makes space.]</p> <p>[Then she holds out her hand.]</p> <p>Aleena: Hey.</p> <p>[Elia makes a sound that could be a sob, could be a laugh.]</p> <p>Aleena: You hurt.</p> <p>Aleena: That&#x27;s real.</p> <p>Aleena: But you&#x27;re alive.</p> <p>[Elia stares at the offered hand like it&#x27;s a myth.]</p> <p>Aleena: You can stay.</p> <p>Aleena: Or you can come with me.</p> <p>[A pause.]</p> <p>Aleena: No penalty.</p> <p>[Elia doesn&#x27;t know what &quot;penalty&quot; means.]</p> <p>[She knows what &quot;come with me&quot; means.]</p> <p>[It means there is a direction.]</p> <p>[A floor.]</p> <p>[Elia reaches out with shaking fingers.]</p> <p>[Aleena closes her hand around Elia&#x27;s wrist, not tight, not taking, just... confirming contact.]</p> <p>Aleena: You coming?</p> <p>[Elia doesn&#x27;t have words.]</p> <p>[Elia nods.]</p> <hr> <p>## Return - Maintenance Bay</p> <p>[Elia&#x27;s gaze snaps back to Avyanna like she realized she left the room for too long.]</p> <p>Elia: She found me in wreckage.</p> <p>Elia: Asked.</p> <p>Elia: Didn&#x27;t take.</p> <p>[Avyanna&#x27;s throat tightens.]</p> <p>Avyanna: She said &quot;you coming?&quot;</p> <p>Elia: [a small nod] Yeah.</p> <p>Elia: That&#x27;s the whole thing.</p> <p>Elia: It&#x27;s not a command.</p> <p>Elia: It&#x27;s a door.</p> <p>[Elia picks up the cloth again, wipes a blade edge with ritual care.]</p> <p>Elia: Sometimes you hold the door.</p> <p>Elia: Sometimes you walk through it.</p> <p>Elia: Sometimes you kick it down.</p> <p>[She looks up, eyes bright with something that might be grief and might be joy.]</p> <p>Elia: But it&#x27;s always your feet.</p> <p>[Avyanna swallows.]</p> <p>Avyanna: Okay.</p> <p>Elia: [crooked] Good.</p> <p>Elia: Now go do whatever your vibrating problem is.</p> <p>Avyanna: [deadpan] That was emotional.</p> <p>Elia: [immediately defensive] It was practical.</p> <p>[Avyanna walks out of the bay with her chest tight and her spine a fraction straighter.]</p> <p>[A door, offered.]</p> <p>[A phrase that means: choose.]</p> <hr> <p>*End Chapter 20d*</p> </div> * [[Finish Act 1->Act 1 Ending]] * [[Return to route map->Act 1 Route Map]]
